title: Filling a cavity through a reservoir and a feed-channel and related apparatus and methods
abstract: Aspects of the present application provide an optical device comprising a suspended optical component over a cavity, such as an undercut region in a substrate. The cavity is filled with a filler material. In some embodiments, the optical device and a method may be provided to fill the cavity with the filler material using a reservoir and a channel in the substrate connecting the reservoir to the cavity to be filled.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An optical device, comprising: a substrate; an optical component suspended above a cavity in the substrate; a reservoir in the substrate; a channel of smaller cross-section than the reservoir connecting the reservoir with the cavity; and a cured filler material disposed in the cavity and in contact with the optical component. 2. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the optical component is an optical waveguide. 3. The optical device of claim 2, wherein the optical waveguide is surrounded by the cured filler material on at least three sides. 4. The optical device of claim 1, further comprising a layer disposed above the cavity and having a plurality of openings to the cavity formed therein. 5. The optical device of claim 4, wherein the optical component is suspended above the cavity by a plurality of tethers defined by at least some of the plurality of openings in the layer disposed above the cavity. 6. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the cross-section of the channel has a width of between 10 Î¼Î¹Î· and 50 Î¼Î·Î¹ and the cross-section of the reservoir has a width of at least 50 Î¼Î·Î¹. 7. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the cured filler material is a dielectric material. 8. The optical device of claim 1, wherein the optical component is a first optical component and the cavity is a first cavity, wherein the substrate further comprises a second optical component suspended above a second cavity, wherein the cured filler material is 

 disposed in both the first and second cavities and in contact with the first and second optical components, and wherein the channel connects the first and second cavities with the reservoir. 9. An optical device, comprising: an optical waveguide, the optical waveguide having a first propagation direction; a cavity in a substrate underneath the optical waveguide; and a cured filler material disposed in the cavity, in contact with the optical waveguide on at least one side, wherein a number density of bubbles in the cavity with a volume of at least 1x10 - " 18 m 3 is no more than 1 per 500 Î¼ÏÎ¹ along the first propagation direction. 10. The optical device of claim 9, further comprising a layer disposed above the cavity and having a plurality of openings to the cavity formed therein. 11. The optical device of claim 10, wherein the optical waveguide is suspended above the cavity by a plurality of tethers defined by at least some of the plurality of openings in the layer disposed above the cavity. 12. The optical device of claim 9, wherein the cured filler material is a dielectric material. 13. The optical device of claim 9, wherein the optical waveguide is a first optical waveguide and the cavity is a first cavity, wherein the optical device further comprises a second optical waveguide suspended above a second cavity, wherein the cured filler material is disposed in both the first and second cavities and in contact with the first and second optical waveguides. 14. A method, comprising: disposing a flowable material in a reservoir of a wafer; flowing the flowable material to a cavity in the wafer via a channel, wherein an optical component is suspended above the cavity; and solidifying the flowable material into a filler material in contact with the optical structure. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the optical component is an optical waveguide, and wherein solidifying the flowable material comprises solidifying the flowable material in contact with the optical waveguide. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein flowing the flowable material comprises surrounding the waveguide by the flowable material on at least three sides. 17. The method of claim 14, wherein flowing the flowable material comprises flowing the flowable material from the reservoir to the cavity via the channel under capillary effect. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the filler material is a flowable dielectric material. 19. The method of claim 14, wherein the cavity is formed in a substrate, and the channel and the reservoir are disposed in the substrate and common to the cavity. 20. The method of claim 15, further comprising dicing the wafer along a plane to expose a facet of the optical waveguide in the plane, wherein the facet is in contact with the filler material on at least three sides in the plane.
